title: Merge Intervals
description:
## Merge Intervals

Given an array of intervals where `intervals[i] = [start_i, end_i]`, merge all overlapping intervals and return an array of the non-overlapping intervals that cover all the intervals in the input.

**Constraints:**
- `1 <= intervals.length <= 10^4`
- `intervals[i].length == 2`
- `0 <= start_i <= end_i <= 10^4`

examples.explanation: [1,3] and [2,6] overlap → merge to [1,6]. Others don't overlap.

solutions.label: Sort and Merge — O(n log n)
solutions.explanation: Sort intervals by start time. Then scan linearly: if the current interval's start is ≤ the last merged interval's end, they overlap — extend the last interval's end. Otherwise, add the current interval as a new non-overlapping segment. Sorting ensures we only ever need to compare with the last merged interval.

solutions.label: Brute Force — O(n² log n)
solutions.explanation: Repeatedly pass through the sorted list until no more merges happen. In each pass, merge any overlapping consecutive intervals. This is inefficient since one pass is always enough after sorting — but it illustrates the concept of iteratively reducing overlaps. The optimal solution does it in a single pass.

solutions.label: Sort and Merge — O(n log n)
solutions.explanation: Sort intervals by start time. Then scan linearly: if the current interval's start is <= the last merged interval's end, they overlap — extend the end. Otherwise, add as a new segment.

hints: Sort intervals by start time. Then scan left to right: if the next interval overlaps with the last one, merge them.
Two intervals [a,b] and [c,d] overlap if c <= b (c starts before b ends). Merge by extending end to max(b, d).
